html:
Exclusive to:

Poison Dartfrog Legs are a Meat Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve: Hamlet DLC. Eating Poison Dartfrog Legs raw or cooked will restore a little bit of hunger, though it will poison the player. It also cannot be used as an ingredient in the Crock Pot.

They can be given to a Bird in a Birdcage to produce an Egg.

Drying raw Poison Dartfrog Legs can turn them into Small Jerky and remove the poison.

Another use of this item is to make the Venom Gland, which can be crafted with 3 raw Poison Dartfrog Legs.

Cooked Dartfrog Legs

Usage

×3

Tips

  • One can dry a pair of Poison Dartfrog Legs on a Drying Rack to remove their poisoning effects and gain the benefits from a Small Jerky, while it can also used as a cooking ingredient in a Crock Pot.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

“Looks noxious.”
–Wilson
“I'm not sure I should eat that.”
–Willow
“Is danger froggy parts!”
–Wolfgang
“These poisonous legs will hasten my demise.”
–Wendy
“WARNING: LEG MECHANISMS CONTAIN TOXIN”
–WX-78
“Agalychnis callidryas is not poisonous, but it may not be Agalychnis callidryas.”
–Wickerbottom
“Those legs look toxic.”
–Woodie
“A delicacy, were it not poisoned.”
–Maxwell
“Observations lead me to conclude this will cause me harm.”
–Wagstaff
“Its meat hath been tainted!”
–Wigfrid
“I'm not sure these are edible like this.”
–Webber
“I don't know why, but I don't think I should eat those yet.”
–Walani
“It be best to cook these before eating.”
–Warly
“Best b'careful eatin' thet!”
–Woodlegs
“TOE O' FROG”
–Wilba
“(sniff, sniff) Smells bad”
–Wormwood
“How adventurous am I feeling today?”
–Wheeler
Poison Dartfrog Legs are a Meat Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve: Hamlet DLC. Eating Poison Dartfrog Legs raw or cooked will restore a little bit of hunger, though it will poison the player. It also cannot be used as an ingredient in the Crock Pot.
They can be given to a Bird in a Birdcage to produce an Egg.
Drying raw Poison Dartfrog Legs can turn them into Small Jerky and remove the poison.
Another use of this item is to make the Venom Gland, which can be crafted with 3 raw Poison Dartfrog Legs.
Cooked Dartfrog Legs[edit | edit source]
“Tainted fricassee.”
–Wilson
“Only one way to find out if it's still poison...”
–Willow
“Even danger food make Wolfgang strong.”
–Wolfgang
“One can only hope the poison has not left.”
–Wendy
“BURNT TOXIC LEG MECHANISM”
–WX-78
“I'm not convinced the poison has left.”
–Wickerbottom
“I don't know, eh...?”
–Woodie
“Shall I risk it?”
–Maxwell
“Relatively less detrimental than before.”
–Wagstaff
“Cautiön tö the wind, I shall cönsume thee!”
–Wigfrid
“I wonder if all the poison is out of them now.”
–Webber
“Looks a little tastier.”
–Walani
“Should I risk eating these now?”
–Warly
“Ain't no proper meal.”
–Woodlegs
“FWOOSH'D TOE O' FROG”
–Wilba
“(sniff, sniff) Smells okay”
–Wormwood
“Meat on a stick. Well... a bone.”
–Wheeler
Usage[edit | edit source]
×3
Tips[edit | edit source]
One can dry a pair of Poison Dartfrog Legs on a Drying Rack to remove their poisoning effects and gain the benefits from a Small Jerky, while it can also used as a cooking ingredient in a Crock Pot.

'''Poison Dartfrog Legs''' are a [[Meat]] [[Food]] [[Item]] exclusive to ''[[Hamlet|Don't Starve: Hamlet]]'' DLC. Eating Poison Dartfrog Legs raw or cooked will restore a little bit of hunger, though it will [[poison]] the player. It also cannot be used as an ingredient in the [[Crock Pot]].

They can be given to a [[Birds|Bird]] in a [[Birdcage]] to produce an [[Egg]].

Drying raw Poison Dartfrog Legs can turn them into [[Small Jerky]] and remove the poison.

Another use of this item is to make the [[Venom Gland]], which can be crafted with 3 raw Poison Dartfrog Legs.

==Cooked Dartfrog Legs==

{{Recipe

|count1 = 1
|tool = Drying Rack
|result = Small Jerky}}

== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==

* One can dry a pair of Poison Dartfrog Legs on a Drying Rack to remove their poisoning effects and gain the benefits from a Small Jerky, while it can also used as a cooking ingredient in a Crock Pot.
